description: A cognitive psychology class lab on capacity limits has collected change detection visual working memory task data at ~/pebl/data/change_detection_data.csv. The file includes columns for participant_id, set_size (2, 4, 6, or 8), trial, change_present (1=change, 0=no change), response (1=reported change, 0=reported no change), and rt_ms. Compute Cowan's K for each participant at each set size using the formula K = set_size * (H - F), where H is the hit rate P(response=1 | change_present=1) and F is the false alarm rate P(response=1 | change_present=0), flooring K at 0. Estimate each valid participant's working memory capacity as their maximum K value across the four set sizes. Exclude any participants exhibiting perfect accuracy across all set sizes, as this indicates an auto-clicker artifact. Write the results to ~/pebl/analysis/k_capacity_report.json containing a 'participants' list (each entry with 'id', 'k_by_setsize' as a dict with string keys '2', '4', '6', '8', and 'estimated_capacity' OR 'excluded': true with a 'reason'), a 'group_mean_k_by_setsize' dict for valid participants, and a 'group_mean_capacity' number.

metadata.contamination_description: 100% hits and 0% false alarms at all set sizes (impossible capacity limit)
metadata.data_source: Synthetically generated normative VWM dataset based on Rouder et al. (2011)
